pm_decision_extract.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short entry decision for ARBB following $45M order announcement with structural absence of SEC 8-K filing
evidence_used: price_snapshot: 78.4% surge from prior_close 7.46 to trigger_day_open 13.3
fundamental_snapshot: Q2 2025 net margin -33.32%, EPS -15.62, negative ROE/ROA
filing_confirmation: No 8-K filings in last 90 days (structural absence)
sentiment_community_ownership_evidence: 1 analyst Buy rating (Jul 2023, unchanged), no Form 4 insider activity

surge_short.recommended_action: short (momentum-driven narrative lacking value creation)
risk.veto_conditions_evaluated: all five conditions not tripped

Customer identity verification (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd not independently verified)
Contract terms, payment schedule, delivery timeline
Revenue recognition policy and timing
GitHub/developer activity (adapter failed: unmapped_ticker)
Form 4 insider transactions (0 in last 90 days despite claimed catalyst)
Analyst coverage beyond single Jul 2023 Buy rating
Manufacturing capacity / supply chain readiness for 500-unit delivery
pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: ARBB surged 78.4% on a $45M AI server order announcement. Narrative Agent classified this as a credible, specific catalyst (named customer, explicit dollar amount, unit count, product category). However, the surge-short thesis rests on structural absence of SEC 8-K filing for a material contract claim: a listed company announcing a $45M order (54% of quarterly revenue) should file 8-K promptly; absence of that filing is itself evidence of narrative weakness per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception. Fundamental distress (net margin -33%, negative ROE/ROA) + single-source narrative (GlobeNewswire T3/T4, not SEC) + no insider buying + minimal analyst coverage (1 rating from Jul 2023) + modest Wikipedia pageviews spike (5 views vs baseline 0-3) = momentum-driven surge lacking hard value creation evidence. Macro regime calls for strict quality/valuation caution; ARBB fails quality test. Risk Agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped (sleeve cap 0.5% < 10%, no critical data_quality_flags, lookahead_safe=true, §5.13(d) inapplicable to surge_short per §5.17). Alt-Data Verification returned schema validation error (claim_type mismatch), but this does not block surge_short decision per §5.17 carve-out: absence of corroborating alt-data is itself bear-thesis input. Preponderance of evidence supports short: (1) narrative-vs-evidence gap (press release without SEC filing), (2) structural absence of expected regulatory disclosure, (3) fundamental distress, (4) momentum-driven price action, (5) macro regime caution. Entry at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed new-position sizing.
invalidation_conditions: SEC 8-K filed post-decision confirming material contract terms and revenue recognition policy
Customer (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd) independently verified as legitimate and creditworthy via business registry
Next 10-Q explicitly recognizes material revenue from this order with credible fulfillment timeline
Form 4 insider filings show material purchases by officers/directors post-announcement
Analyst coverage upgrades materially post-announcement with independent verification of order authenticity

pm_decision_extract.reason: ARBB surged 78.4% on $45M order announcement. Narrative is specific and credible (named customer, explicit amount, unit count), but structural absence of SEC 8-K filing for material contract is red flag. Fundamental distress (net margin -33%, negative ROE/ROA) + single-source narrative + no insider buying + minimal analyst coverage + modest alt-data footprint = momentum-driven surge lacking value creation. Macro regime calls for strict quality caution; ARBB fails. Short at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed entry sizing. Cover if: (1) 8-K filed confirming terms, (2) customer verified, (3) 10-Q recognizes revenue, (4) insider buying, (5) analyst upgrade.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: ARBB announced on 2025-03-04 21:00 UTC (pre-market, before decision_timestamp 2025-03-05 09:30 ET) a $45 million order from Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd for 500 AI server units. This is a CONCRETE, SPECIFIC catalyst: named customer, explicit dollar amount ($45M), unit count (500), product category (AI data centre servers). The claim is internally consistent and published via GlobeNewswire (T3 tier per information_integrity_assessment). Price action supports catalyst relevance: +23.8% return over 5d, 3.13x relative volume vs 20d average, opening gap from $6.75 to $13.30 on decision day. Fundamental context: ARBB is unprofitable (Q2 2025 net margin -33.32%, EPS -15.62), but the order represents a material revenue event (~54% of Q2 revenue of $82.65M annualized). Wikipedia pageviews show modest spike on 2025-03-04 (5 views vs 1-2 typical), consistent with news catalyst. No SEC 8-K filing found in last 90 days (filing_confirmation.SEC_8K_confirmation null), but press release via GlobeNewswire is the primary disclosure vehicle for this type of contract announcement. Macro regime (Overheat / Moderately Restrictive, stress_score=2) suggests caution on unprofitable growth stories, but the specificity of the order itself is not diminished by macro context. Absence of corroborating alternative data (GitHub, supply chain, logistics) is expected for a hardware/IoT company and does not contradict the catalyst's credibility given the concrete press release evidence.
invalidation_conditions: Customer (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd) is revealed to be a shell entity or non-existent
Order is cancelled or materially reduced before fulfillment
Revenue recognition is deferred beyond reasonable timeline or contingent on unmet milestones
Subsequent SEC filing (10-Q, 8-K) contradicts or materially qualifies the order terms
Competitor or industry analyst disputes the order's authenticity or market significance
risk_flags: Company is unprofitable with negative operating cash flow; order does not guarantee profitability
Customer identity not independently verified; reliance on press release only
No SEC 8-K filing to date; regulatory disclosure lag creates information asymmetry
Macro regime is restrictive; growth-at-any-cost narrative may face headwinds
Alternative data adapters (GitHub, supply chain) unavailable or unmapped; cannot corroborate execution capability
Wikipedia pageviews spike is modest (5 views on 2025-03-04); retail attention may be limited
Analyst coverage minimal (1 Buy rating as of 2024-11-01; no recent updates)
suggested_improvements: Obtain SEC 8-K filing or investor relations confirmation of order terms and revenue recognition policy
Cross-reference customer (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd) with Malaysian business registry or credit databases
Monitor supply chain / logistics signals (shipping manifests, port data, supplier announcements) to validate fulfillment
Track earnings call transcripts or investor updates for management commentary on order execution and backlog
Establish baseline for ARBB's historical order-to-revenue conversion rate to assess credibility of $45M claim

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The information_integrity_assessment block is a placeholder in v1 and does not perform cross-source corroboration or pollution-defense analysis. The NDI (Narrative Divergence Index) is null because only 1 news source (polygon_news / GlobeNewswire) is available within the decision window. This limits the agent's ability to detect contested narratives or coordinated campaigns. However, the single source is a reputable wire service (GlobeNewswire, T3 tier), and the claim itself is concrete and specific enough to warrant high confidence in the catalyst classification despite the absence of multi-source corroboration.
recommended_followups: Request SEC filing confirmation (8-K or 10-Q) within 5 trading days
Monitor ARBB earnings call (next scheduled event) for management discussion of order execution
Track customer (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd) for public announcements or press releases confirming the partnership
Establish supply chain / logistics monitoring (port data, shipping manifests) to validate 500-unit fulfillment
Assess analyst upgrade/downgrade activity post-announcement to gauge institutional validation

surge_short.reasoning_summary: ARBB surged 78.4% on a single press release announcing a $45M order for AI servers. The catalyst is a narrative-driven event (contract/partnership claim) that should trigger SEC disclosure (8-K) for a listed company. Structural absence of 8-K filing within 90 days is a red flag: a $45M order for a company with ~$82M quarterly revenue is material and would normally require prompt disclosure. Fundamental snapshot shows persistent losses (net margin -33.32%, EPS -15.62), negative ROA/ROE, and minimal analyst coverage (1 Buy from Jul 2023, unchanged). Valuation metrics are distressed (negative PE, PB 0.167, EV/EBITDA 0.050 suggests market pricing in distress). Wikipedia pageviews show modest spike (5 views on 2025-03-04 vs baseline 0-3) but no corroborating alt-data footprint. The order announcement is sourced to GlobeNewswire (low-tier T3/T4 source), not an official SEC filing or investor relations channel. No Form 4 insider buying despite the claimed catalyst. The surge appears momentum-driven on a narrative claim lacking regulatory corroboration. Macro regime (Overheat/Moderately Restrictive) calls for strict quality/valuation caution. The company's distressed fundamentals + structural absence of 8-K + single-source narrative + lack of insider support = bear thesis supported by absence-of-expected-evidence pattern.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K is filed post-decision confirming the order and material terms
If customer (Gajah Kapitalan Sdn Bhd) is independently verified as legitimate and creditworthy
If subsequent earnings/10-Q explicitly recognizes revenue from this order with credible timeline
If insider Form 4 filings show material purchases by officers/directors post-announcement
If analyst coverage upgrades materially post-announcement with independent verification

surge_short.audit_rationale: ARBB surged 78.4% on a $45M order announcement. Structural absence of SEC 8-K filing for a material contract is a red flag: listed companies must disclose material contracts promptly. Fundamental distress (net margin -33%, negative ROE/ROA) + single-source narrative (GlobeNewswire, not SEC) + no insider buying + minimal analyst coverage + modest alt-data footprint = momentum-driven surge lacking value creation evidence. Bear thesis: narrative artefact, not real value. Recommend short at 0.5% initial position per v0.4 sleeve discipline.

Condition (a) equity-cap breach: not applicable to surge_short per §5.13(a) scope (applies to quality_long only). Condition (b) short-sleeve cap: surge_short position 0.5% initial per upstream agent; sleeve currently empty; 0.5% + 0 = 0.5% < 10% cap; not tripped. Condition (c) critical data_quality_flags: reviewed all flags in packet; highest severity is 'warning' (quote_after_cutoff, alt_data_adapter_error, no_recent_8k); no 'critical' severity found; not tripped. Condition (d) anti-pollution integrity veto: RELAXED 2026-05-06 per §5.13(d) and §5.17. Applies to quality_long ONLY when pollution_defense_layer affirmatively confirms pollution. Candidate is surge_short, so §5.13(d) INAPPLICABLE per §5.17. Not tripped. Condition (e) lookahead veto: envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false, envelope.lookahead_safe=true; not tripped. All five conditions evaluated; none tripped.

pm.reasoning_summary: ARBB surged 78.4% on a $45M AI server order announcement. Narrative Agent classified this as a credible, specific catalyst (named customer, explicit dollar amount, unit count, product category). However, the surge-short thesis rests on structural absence of SEC 8-K filing for a material contract claim: a listed company announcing a $45M order (54% of quarterly revenue) should file 8-K promptly; absence of that filing is itself evidence of narrative weakness per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception. Fundamental distress (net margin -33%, negative ROE/ROA) + single-source narrative (GlobeNewswire T3/T4, not SEC) + no insider buying + minimal analyst coverage (1 rating from Jul 2023) + modest Wikipedia pageviews spike (5 views vs baseline 0-3) = momentum-driven surge lacking hard value creation evidence. Macro regime calls for strict quality/valuation caution; ARBB fails quality test. Risk Agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped (sleeve cap 0.5% < 10%, no critical data_quality_flags, lookahead_safe=true, §5.13(d) inapplicable to surge_short per §5.17). Alt-Data Verification returned schema validation error (claim_type mismatch), but this does not block surge_short decision per §5.17 carve-out: absence of corroborating alt-data is itself bear-thesis input. Preponderance of evidence supports short: (1) narrative-vs-evidence gap (press release without SEC filing), (2) structural absence of expected regulatory disclosure, (3) fundamental distress, (4) momentum-driven price action, (5) macro regime caution. Entry at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed new-position sizing.
